description_long: This stone castle features four prominent cylindrical corner towers, each topped with battlements and slightly flared parapets. The main structure is rectangular, with high crenellated walls connecting the towers and enclosing a central courtyard. The stonework is rough and weathered, giving the castle a robust, medieval appearance. The front facade includes a large arched entranceway, suggesting access to the interior courtyard. The towers are evenly spaced and rise above the main walls, providing elevated vantage points. The overall design is defensive, with thick walls and minimal decorative elements, emphasizing function over ornamentation.
description: A robust stone castle with four cylindrical corner towers, high crenellated walls, and a central arched entrance. The structure is medieval in style, with rough stonework and a fortified appearance.
description_view_0: The castle is seen from the rear, showing the four towers and the open central courtyard, with no visible entrance.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the symmetry of the towers and the solid stone walls, with the courtyard visible inside.
description_view_2: Another rear-side angle, showing the towers and battlements, with the courtyard and wall details clearly visible.
description_view_3: The front view displays the main arched entrance at the base of the central wall, flanked by the two front towers.
